title: Migrate model dependency downloads from Hugging Face to Cloudflare R2
description:
Move all MPI-owned re-hosted model weights off Hugging Face onto Cloudflare R2 and repoint every dependency/download URL at R2. ROOT CAUSE: long-lived HF/Xet streams degrade in waves (sawtooth 40MB/s -> sub-MB/s -> climbs back) during large local downloads (observed live on ltx23-gemma-clip, MPI-127). The in-app slow-stream reconnect watchdog (commit 02ef2c0) correctly rides out self-recovering dips and does NOT fix the throttling. R2 fixes it at the source: free unlimited egress, own CDN, flat throughput, download-volume-proof bill (~$0.75/mo storage for ~60GB, NO per-download cost no matter how many users pull). Use R2 Standard, NOT Infrequent Access (IA's $0.01/GB retrieval fee defeats the point for user-facing weights). TARGET (UPDATED 2026-06-27, MPI-137): models go to the DEDICATED bucket **cubric-models**, host **https://models.cubric.studio/vision/<type>/<file>** (NOT cubric-builds/models/ — that was the release-night expedient MPI-137 already undid). Same rclone remote `cubric-r2:` + token (now scoped to cubric-builds + cubric-models + cubric-pod-runtime; ~/.secrets/rclone-r2.conf, no conf change). Layout: cubric-models/vision/<type>/<file> so other Cubric apps get their own top-level prefix. WORK ITEMS: (1) PUBLISH HELPER — a documented rclone push to cubric-models/vision/ (mirror the pattern in mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/publish-runtime.sh / the R2 capability doc). (2+3) REMAINING UPLOADS + URL SWAP — as of 2026-06-27 the LTX-2.3 set is DONE (gemma-clip + 2 VAEs + text-projection + spatial-upscaler + 3 baked LoRAs are on cubric-models/vision/ltx-2.3/, URLs swapped in dependencies.js + the builder install script — MPI-137). The 9 STILL-ON-HF MPI re-hosts remain: SDXL_Realistic, SDXL_NSFW, ILL_Anime, ILL_Anime_Beauty, PONY_Mix (5 checkpoints) + Wan_22_t2v_High/Low + Wan_22_i2v_High/Low (4 Wan). For each: rclone copy from huggingface.co/Mad-Pony-Interactive/cubric-studio -> cubric-models/vision/<type>/, swap the url in js/data/modelConstants/dependencies.js to https://models.cubric.studio/vision/..., verify sha256 + HEAD-200. (0 stale dl.cubric.studio refs remain.) SCOPE: only MPI-OWNED re-hosts move. Upstream files NOT ours (Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy base, Lightricks upscaler) stay on HF unless MPI-128 item 2 self-hosts them — then to R2 (cubric-models/vision), not HF. CROSS-REF MPI-128 (item 2) + MPI-137 (bucket + LTX subset done). DEFERRED from 2026-06-25 session to avoid blocking tonight's release. RELEASE-GATING: tonight's drop is Patreon/Pro-only (0 subscribers -> safe without R2). The FIRST PUBLIC GitHub release ships from master in ~2 weeks and MUST have R2 links for fast downloads. So this card ALSO requires: (a) land R2 dep URLs on master, (b) watchdog needs NO master action - verified master never had it (add 02ef2c0 + disable a780e0a both RunPod-only), so master never had the crash; just confirm public build has no live watchdog, (c) bump master 1.0.1 -> v1.0.2 for the public launch. See brief.md 'Release targets' section.

=== STATUS 2026-06-28 (read THIS first, it's current) ===
DONE: LTX-2.3 set fully on R2 (cubric-models/vision/ltx-2.3/, URLs swapped). PATH CONVENTION (confirmed live): vision/<model-set>/<comfy-subdir>/<file>, e.g. vision/ltx-2.3/diffusion_models/...  rclone remote `cubric-r2:`, config ~/.secrets/rclone-r2.conf — MUST pass RCLONE_CONFIG=~/.secrets/rclone-r2.conf (default rclone.conf absent on this box). Verify with rclone (lsl/size), NOT curl (schannel flakes).
WAN MIGRATION (2026-06-28): DO NOT re-download Wan from HF. All 4 Wan files are ALREADY ON LOCAL DISK at G:/CubricModels/diffusion_models/Wan_22_{t2v,i2v}_{High,Low}.safetensors. The migration is a simple LOCAL->R2 rclone push (upload leg = flat/fast ~200MB/s, NOT the throttled HF download leg). Command per file: RCLONE_CONFIG=~/.secrets/rclone-r2.conf rclone copy G:/CubricModels/diffusion_models/Wan_22_t2v_High.safetensors cubric-r2:cubric-models/vision/wan-2.2/diffusion_models/ -P. Target prefix vision/wan-2.2/diffusion_models/<file>; host https://models.cubric.studio/vision/wan-2.2/diffusion_models/<file>. After upload: swap dependencies.js wan-22-{t2v,i2v}-{high,low} url lines (sha256 unchanged, byte-identical), verify with rclone lsl (NOT curl). NOTE the earlier HF-download approach was a MISTAKE (files were already local) — killed. R2 status as of this edit: nothing in vision/wan-2.2/ yet.
WHY THE DOWNLOAD HANGS (user observed live, RTX 4090 remote Pod, app download manager stuck 33.6/36.5GB @ 4.8MB/s): same HF/Xet tail-throttle the LTX 41GB transformer also hit. REGRESSION SUSPECT — user says Wan downloaded FINE before, broke recently: commit a780e0a DISABLED the slow-stream reconnect watchdog (it crashed the server on a bad speed reading). The watchdog used to force-reconnect throttled HF streams back to full speed; with it dead, a throttled tail just crawls at ~5MB/s forever instead of being kicked. R2 fixes this at the source (no throttle) regardless. If staying on HF were ever needed, the watchdog regression (a780e0a) is the thing to fix. NOT yet investigated/confirmed — flagged for whoever picks this up.
STILL PENDING after Wan t2v: Wan i2v High/Low (2 files) + 5 SDXL/IL/PONY checkpoints. Then (a) land R2 URLs on master, (b) confirm public build has no live watchdog, (c) bump master 1.0.1->1.0.2 for public launch.
=== END STATUS ===
